visual_features.overall_style: Clean, modern marketing layout with a light, airy feel. Mix of vacation-resort branding (script logo, palm tree icon) and straightforward, minimal UI elements. Strong use of a single brand green on a mostly white canvas, with soft shadows and rounded cards giving a relaxed, approachable tone.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Top-contact bar (high contrast green) draws attention first, followed by logo and primary navigation on white. The active nav item 'Packages' is emphasized via green text and an underline bar. Below, the grid/list toggle sits left as a minor control, while the main content focus is the large property card: big photo first, then bold title, then secondary pricing text. Hierarchy is created via size (photo > title > price), weight (bold italic title vs light price), and color (green for active states and price).
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Very generous whitespace, especially to the right of the single property card and between header/nav and content. Sections are separated by large vertical gaps rather than rules. Card content has ample internal padding and breathing room, reinforcing a calm, premium, vacation feel.
unique_elements: Script-style logo with palm tree icon in brand green
Active nav item indicated by a slim green underline bar below the label
Grid/list toggle rendered as two square icon buttons with borders on a large white field
Large, photo-first property card with soft, diffused drop shadow and fully rounded corners

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implied 12-column responsive grid with a centered content container; card width suggests using 3–4 columns on desktop with large outer gutters.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width header and navigation, followed by a centered content area. Content section uses a left-aligned column for controls and cards, with large empty space on the right (likely filled by more cards at other breakpoints).
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top thin contact bar; below it a primary navigation/header bar; then a main content area with view toggle controls, followed by a grid of property cards. Sections are stacked vertically and visually separated by whitespace and background color changes (green bar vs white).
layout_characteristics.alignment: Globally left-aligned content within a centered max-width container. Navigation items are horizontally centered in the header region, while logo hugs the left. Text inside cards is left-aligned. Icons and controls sit on a consistent left margin.
responsive_hints: Large left gutter and right empty space indicate that multiple cards can appear in a row on wider viewports and collapse to fewer columns on smaller screens.
Grid/list toggle indicates alternative layouts for smaller devices (list view likely a single column).
Top contact bar and nav bar are full-width and can easily adapt to mobile via stacking icons and collapsing navigation to a menu.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: full-width contact strip, then full-width logo + horizontal navigation, then a main white content area. Inside the content area, small control buttons (grid/list toggle) are aligned near the left margin, followed by a vertically stacked set of property cards. Each card is image-first with a full-width photo on top, then a text block with title and price beneath.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Top contact bar is shallow (~10–12% of header height) but full-width and dense. The main nav/header section occupies roughly another 15–20% of the initial viewport height. The remaining viewport is dominated by the content area, where each property card takes a moderate width but a significant vertical dimension due to its large photo. A single card currently uses the left ~30–40% of the content width, leaving generous blank space on the right (for additional cards in more populated states). Large vertical margins separate header, controls, and cards.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Spacious layout with ample margins, large padding in cards, and minimal on-screen elements at once. Information density is low and intentionally minimal, aligning with an editorial, lifestyle marketing approach rather than a utilitarian listing grid.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Strong vertical flow: eye starts at the high-contrast green contact bar, then moves down to the logo and navigation. The active 'Packages' label and its green underline pull focus next. From there, the eye descends to the grid/list toggle controls on the left, then naturally to the large property card image, and finally down to its title and price. Overall, it’s a top-to-bottom, slightly left-biased F-pattern.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Grid/list toggle buttons are square (~40x40px) with sharp or very slightly rounded 2–4px corners, white fill, and a light 1px grey border (#D9D9D9). Icons inside (grid and list) are dark grey. No visible drop shadow; flat design with plenty of white space around. Primary CTAs elsewhere would likely use the brand green fill, white text, and similar border radius for consistency.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Implemented as <button> or <a> elements styled as buttons, wrapped in a flex container for horizontal alignment. Each button contains an <i> or <svg> icon element. Active state would be represented via an additional class toggling background and border color (e.g., .btn-toggle--active).

ui_patterns.visual_description: Property card is a rounded rectangle with a noticeable radius (~12–16px), white background, and a soft diffuse drop shadow creating elevation (e.g., 0 8px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)). No visible border; separation is handled by shadow and spacing. The top half is dominated by a full-bleed image whose top corners share the card’s radius; bottom corners are square where it meets the text area. The text area has generous padding (~20–24px). Title is bold, dark, and possibly italic; price line is smaller, green, and spaced slightly below the title.

with an at the top (card__image) followed by a
containing a heading (e.g.,

or

) and a paragraph or span for price. The entire card may be wrapped in a link () or use an overlay link to make the card clickable.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No full form is visible in this screenshot, but pattern inferred from inputs elsewhere on similar sites: simple rectangular inputs with slight rounding (4–6px), light grey border, and white background. Labels likely sit above inputs for clarity. Error/success states would use colored borders (red/green) and small helper text beneath fields. Overall density would be moderate with vertical stacking and ample spacing.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Stacked labels with full-width inputs in a single-column layout: <label> above <input> or <select>, grouped in <div class="form-group"> wrappers. Validation messages rendered as small <span class="form-error"> elements below each input. Form sections may use <fieldset> and <legend> for semantic grouping when needed.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary nav is a horizontal text menu on a white background. Items are medium-sized, dark grey text with generous horizontal spacing (~24–32px). The active item uses the brand green for text and has a slim green underline bar positioned directly beneath the label, spanning the text width. No visible borders or background fills on inactive items, for a clean, flat appearance.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented as a <nav> with an unordered list (<ul>) of list items (<li>) each containing a link (<a>). Active state controlled via a class on <li> or <a> (e.g., .nav-item--active) to apply green text and the underline pseudo-element. On mobile, the same list could be collapsed into a hamburger-triggered menu.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Property listings behave as a grid of cards rather than a traditional text list. Items are visually separated through card shadows and large gaps instead of lines or rules. The alternative 'list' icon suggests a more condensed vertical layout where cards may narrow and stack, but still keep image + text grouping. Density remains moderate with clear separation between items.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Rendered as a flexbox or CSS grid container (<div class="listing-grid">) with each card as a child <article> or <li>. Grid view uses multiple columns; list view collapses to a single column and may rearrange internal layout into a media-object style (image left, text right) while retaining the same underlying HTML.

heading.visual_style: Headings (e.g., property titles) appear as bold, slightly playful serif or script-like text with italic styling, conveying personality and a resort vibe. Nav labels are a clean sans-serif, so heading style is distinct and more decorative.

body.visual_style: Clean, neutral sans-serif for navigation and informational text: relatively high x-height, good legibility, and not overly condensed. It feels contemporary but unobtrusive.

spacing_system.component_padding: Cards and key components use approximately 16–24px internal padding on all sides, aligning with 2x–3x the 8px base unit.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., nav to content, controls to cards) is roughly 32–48px, giving clear separation and an airy feel.

interaction_hints: Navigation items likely change text color to green and show/retain the underline bar on hover/active; cursor switches to pointer.
Cards may elevate slightly on hover with a stronger box-shadow and possibly a subtle upward translateY transform (2–4px) and smooth 150–250ms transition.
Grid/list toggle buttons change background and/or border color to green for the active view, with icon color turning white or darker for contrast.
Links and pricing text in green may darken or underline on hover, using simple opacity or color transitions.
Any animations should be subtle and ease-based (e.g., ease-out) to maintain the relaxed, vacation-oriented tone.
